before: Recorded in 1999, Detroit/New York Junction showcases Thad Jones at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Hard Bop. Detroit/New York Junction remains a landmark of Hard Bop, cementing Thad Jones's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: Released in 1956 on Blue Note, Detroit-New York Junction is a hard-bop date led by Thad Jones. The program runs through "Blue Room," "Tarriff," "Little Girl Blue," "Scratch," and "Zec."
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz release: title, releaseGroup.firstReleaseDate (1956), Blue Note label, tracklist

before: Recorded in 1987, Dexter Calling showcases Dexter Gordon at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Bebop. Dexter Calling remains a landmark of Bebop, cementing Dexter Gordon's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: First released in 1961 on Blue Note, Dexter Calling is a Dexter Gordon date in the bebop tradition. It includes "Soul Sister," "Modal Mood," "I Want More," "End of a Love Affair," and "Clear the Dex."
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz release: title, releaseGroup.firstReleaseDate (1961-05-09), Blue Note label, tracklist

before: One of Mobley's most soulful outings, built around the irresistible groove of the title track, which became a Blue Note classic. The quintet with Lee Morgan and Harold Mabern Jr. swings with infectious joy throughout. Contains Mobley's most recognizable composition in the title track, a boogaloo-tinged burner that became a staple of jazz DJs and rare groove collectors worldwide.
after: One of Mobley's most soulful outings, built around the irresistible groove of the opener "The Dip," which became a Blue Note classic. The quintet with Lee Morgan and Harold Mabern Jr. swings with infectious joy throughout. "The Dip" is a boogaloo-tinged burner that became a staple of jazz DJs and rare groove collectors worldwide.
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz tracklist (opener 'The Dip'; no track named 'Dippin''); only the phantom title-track claim corrected

before: Recorded in 2004, Drums Unlimited showcases Max Roach at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Bebop. Drums Unlimited remains a landmark of Bebop, cementing Max Roach's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: Released in 1966 on Atlantic, Drums Unlimited is a Max Roach date rooted in the bebop tradition. It includes "The Drum Also Waltzes," "Nommo," the title track, and "For Big Sid."

before: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins is a jazz album by Duke Ellington and Coleman Hawkins that was recorded on August 18, 1962, and released in February 1963 by Impulse! Records. A 1963 big band date for impulse! that documents Duke Ellington's ongoing work in the idiom.
after: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins is a jazz album by Duke Ellington and Coleman Hawkins that was recorded on August 18, 1962, and released in February 1963 by Impulse! Records. An octet date for impulse! that documents Duke Ellington's ongoing work in the idiom.

before: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins is a jazz album by Duke Ellington and Coleman Hawkins that was recorded on August 18, 1962, and released in February 1963 by Impulse! Records. A big band recording from the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication. Coleman Hawkins adds a personal voice to the idiom.
after: Duke Ellington Meets Coleman Hawkins is a jazz album by Duke Ellington and Coleman Hawkins that was recorded on August 18, 1962, and released in February 1963 by Impulse! Records. An octet recording from the hard bop period, reconnecting jazz with blues and gospel while keeping bebop's sophistication. Coleman Hawkins adds a personal voice to the idiom.

before: Pianist Cedar Walton leads a powerhouse quartet with Bob Berg, Sam Jones, and Billy Higgins through a program of his finest compositions. Walton's sophisticated harmonic sense and effortless swing make this one of the greatest straight-ahead jazz piano albums of the 1970s. Proved that hard bop was alive and well during the fusion era. Walton's compositional brilliance—heard in classics like 'Naima's Love Song'—established him as one of jazz's most important composer-pianists.
after: Pianist Cedar Walton leads a powerhouse quartet with Bob Berg, Sam Jones, and Billy Higgins through a program of his finest compositions. Walton's sophisticated harmonic sense and effortless swing make this one of the greatest straight-ahead jazz piano albums of the 1970s. Proved that hard bop was alive and well during the fusion era. Walton's compositional brilliance established him as one of jazz's most important composer-pianists.
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz tracklist has no 'Naima's Love Song'; dropped the unsupported track example, kept the rest verbatim

before: Recorded in 2014, Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Cole Porter Song Book showcases Ella Fitzgerald at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Swing. Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Cole Porter Song Book remains a landmark of Swing, cementing Ella Fitzgerald's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: Released in 1956 on Verve, Ella Fitzgerald Sings the Cole Porter Song Book is one of Fitzgerald's celebrated Song Book sets. It draws on the Cole Porter catalog, including "Anything Goes," "Too Darn Hot," "In the Still of the Night," and "I Get a Kick Out of You."
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz release: title, releaseGroup.firstReleaseDate (1956-09), Verve label, tracklist

before: Recorded in 2016, Far East Suite showcases Duke Ellington at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Swing. Far East Suite remains a landmark of Swing, cementing Duke Ellington's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: Released in 1967 on RCA Victor, The Far East Suite is a Duke Ellington album inspired by his tours abroad. Its pieces include "Tourist Point of View," "Isfahan," "Mount Harissa," and "Blue Pepper (Far East of the Blues)."
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz release: title, releaseGroup.firstReleaseDate (1967), RCA Victor label, tracklist

before: Recorded in 1996, Fontainebleau showcases Tadd Dameron at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Bebop. Fontainebleau remains a landmark of Bebop, cementing Tadd Dameron's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: Recorded March 9, 1956, Fontainebleau is a Tadd Dameron album. The title track is a through-composed piece with no solos, and "Flossie Lou" is a contrafact of "Jeepers Creepers"; the program also includes "Delirium" and "The Scene Is Clean."
evidenceUsed: Wikipedia (entity-matched): recording date March 9, 1956, title-track/Flossie Lou descriptions; tracklist

before: Recorded in 1990, Fontessa showcases Modern Jazz Quartet at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Cool Jazz. Fontessa remains a landmark of Cool Jazz, cementing Modern Jazz Quartet's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: First released in 1956, Fontessa is a Modern Jazz Quartet album in the cool-jazz vein. It includes "Versailles (Porte de Versailles)," "Angel Eyes," the multi-part title piece "Fontessa," and "Willow Weep for Me."
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz release: title, releaseGroup.firstReleaseDate (1956), tracklist (labels empty in evidence)

before: Recorded in 2012, Free for All showcases Art Blakey at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Bebop. Free for All remains a landmark of Bebop, cementing Art Blakey's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: Recorded February 10, 1964, and released on Blue Note in July 1965, Free for All is a studio album by Art Blakey and the Jazz Messengers. It includes the Wayne Shorter title track, "Hammer Head," Freddie Hubbard's "The Core," and "Pensativa."
evidenceUsed: Wikipedia (entity-matched): recorded Feb 10 1964, released Blue Note July 1965, Shorter title track, Hubbard's 'The Core'; tracklist

before: Recorded in 2007, Full House showcases Wes Montgomery at the height of their creative powers, offering one of the finest expressions of Cool Jazz. Full House remains a landmark of Cool Jazz, cementing Wes Montgomery's reputation as one of the defining voices of the era and earning consistent praise from critics worldwide.
after: Full House is a live album by jazz guitarist Wes Montgomery, recorded at Tsubo in Berkeley, California, on June 25, 1962, and released on Riverside. The quintet featured Wynton Kelly, Johnny Griffin, Paul Chambers, and Jimmy Cobb, working through the title track, "Blue 'n' Boogie," and "Cariba."
evidenceUsed: Wikipedia (entity-matched): live, Tsubo Berkeley June 25 1962, Riverside, quintet personnel; MusicBrainz live secondaryType and tracklist

before: Kansas City 8: Get Together is a small-group studio album by Count Basie, released on Pablo Records. Paring down from his famous big band, Basie leads an octet through a relaxed program of swing-rooted originals including "Ode to Pres" and "Basie's Bag," capturing the pianist's spare, economical style in an intimate setting. Pianist Count Basie's post-bop sensibility is on display in this 1986 session, a document of the contemporary era.
after: Kansas City 8: Get Together is a small-group studio album by Count Basie, released on Pablo Records. Paring down from his famous big band, Basie leads an octet through a relaxed program of swing-rooted originals including "Ode to Pres" and "Basie's Bag," capturing the pianist's spare, economical style in an intimate setting. Pianist Count Basie's spare, swinging touch is on display in this 1979 session.
evidenceUsed: Wikipedia (entity-matched): '1979 studio album', corrects '1986 session'; first two sentences kept verbatim
